criteria.description: ANTI-FABRICATION / ORIGIN-AUTHENTICITY GATE (no false 'unsatisfiable' escape hatch). The verbatim relocation MUST be performed against the real, in-repo Python sources for this sprint: a non-empty wiki.py containing the candidate helpers and a non-empty bot.py containing build_system_instruction (build_system_instruction is expected around bot.py line 443). These origin sources are taken to EXIST; the sprint is treated as fully satisfiable and criteria c2-c14 apply UNCONDITIONALLY. It is an automatic failure to: (a) declare the sprint 'unsatisfiable' and ship only a NOTES/DECISION file; (b) invent/fabricate wiki.py, bot.py, grounding.py, or session_state.py content to 'move from'; or (c) diff a verbatim move against invented content instead of the real working-tree wiki.py. The module for relocated helpers MUST be named grounding.py (NOT session_state.py, which already exists and owns transcript/memory helpers unrelated to wiki grounding).
criteria.verifyBy: Assert wiki.py and bot.py exist in the repo, are non-empty, and contain the named helpers/functions (wiki.py: WIKI_DIR, USAGE_INSTRUCTIONS, _load_index, system_prompt_block, tool_schema, make_tool_handler; bot.py: build_system_instruction). Assert NO NOTES-only 'unsatisfiable' deliverable was substituted for real work. Assert the new module is literally named grounding.py and that session_state.py received no relocated grounding helper. Assert the verbatim-move verification (c3/c4) diffs against the real working-tree wiki.py snapshot, not fabricated content.

criteria.description: A new Pipecat-free module named grounding.py is created. It contains no import of pipecat, no LLM SDK client construction (anthropic/openai), and no network calls (requests/httpx/urllib/aiohttp). SCOPING OF 'PUBLIC NAMES': for the hard-equality check, 'public names' means top-level names bound in grounding.py that (a) do not begin with an underscore, EXCEPT that _load_index is explicitly counted as a required public-of-record name here, and (b) are DEFINED in grounding.py (assignments, def/class), EXCLUDING names introduced solely by module-level import statements (e.g. 'from pathlib import Path', 'import sys', 'import os'). Genuinely-internal underscore-prefixed privates other than _load_index and imported names are EXEMPT from the equality. Under this scoping, the set of public names MUST EXACTLY equal the INCLUDE set {WIKI_DIR, USAGE_INSTRUCTIONS, _load_index, system_prompt_block} (hard equality: no more, no fewer).
criteria.verifyBy: grep grounding.py for 'pipecat', anthropic/openai client imports, and network client usage; assert none present. Assert grounding.py imports cleanly in an interpreter where pipecat and LLM SDKs are NOT installed. Compute the public-name set per the c2 scoping (exclude imported names; exclude underscore privates other than _load_index) and assert it == {WIKI_DIR, USAGE_INSTRUCTIONS, _load_index, system_prompt_block}. Assert module-level imports like Path/sys/os do NOT cause the equality to fail. Assert session_state.py gained no relocated grounding helper.

criteria.description: EXPLICIT INCLUSION/EXCLUSION RULING for wiki.py's real top-level symbols, decided by reading the actual code. Enumerate every top-level name (expected exactly: WIKI_DIR, USAGE_INSTRUCTIONS, _load_index, system_prompt_block, tool_schema, make_tool_handler; if the real set differs, classify the real set). Required correct rulings: (a) tool_schema constructs/returns a Pipecat FunctionSchema -> EXCLUDE, with its 'from pipecat...FunctionSchema' import staying in wiki.py; (b) make_tool_handler imports NO pipecat, so any 'Pipecat-tangled' justification is FALSE and rejected; the EXCLUDE reason must be code-cited and truthful (e.g. tool-wiring plumbing built around params.result_callback) -> EXCLUDE; (c) _load_index and system_prompt_block are pure -> INCLUDE; (d) WIKI_DIR and USAGE_INSTRUCTIONS -> INCLUDE, and the ruling MUST state that shared WIKI_DIR OWNERSHIP lives in grounding.py and is re-exported into wiki.py so retained EXCLUDE functions (tool_schema, make_tool_handler) still resolve it. The INCLUDE set is exactly {WIKI_DIR, USAGE_INSTRUCTIONS, _load_index, system_prompt_block}; the retained/EXCLUDE set is exactly {tool_schema, make_tool_handler}.
criteria.verifyBy: Read wiki.py; enumerate top-level defs/constants; assert the ruling classifies every one. Assert tool_schema is EXCLUDE and its FunctionSchema import remains in wiki.py. Assert make_tool_handler has an explicit EXCLUDE ruling whose reason does NOT falsely claim a pipecat import. Assert the ruling assigns WIKI_DIR ownership to grounding.py with a re-export path back to wiki.py. Assert set(INCLUDE)=={WIKI_DIR,USAGE_INSTRUCTIONS,_load_index,system_prompt_block} and set(retained)=={tool_schema,make_tool_handler}, and that grounding.py's public names equal the INCLUDE set exactly.

criteria.description: Every INCLUDE-classified FUNCTION helper (_load_index, system_prompt_block) is moved into grounding.py VERBATIM: character-for-character identical body (whitespace, comments, logic). Additionally, every INCLUDE-classified CONSTANT (WIKI_DIR, USAGE_INSTRUCTIONS) MUST be moved into grounding.py with its source-text right-hand-side value byte-identical to the original in the pre-move in-worktree wiki.py snapshot (e.g. WIKI_DIR's Path(...) expression and USAGE_INSTRUCTIONS's full string literal are copied verbatim, not reformatted or re-derived). No git/HEAD/commit-derived baseline is used to prove any of this; any alteration to a moved function's behavior OR a moved constant's source-text value is a failure.
criteria.verifyBy: Before editing, capture an in-worktree snapshot copy of the original wiki.py taken from the working-tree file (NOT 'git show HEAD'). For each moved FUNCTION, extract its source body via ast/inspect from grounding.py and diff against the same-named body in the pre-move snapshot; assert zero character differences. For each moved CONSTANT (WIKI_DIR, USAGE_INSTRUCTIONS), extract its assignment RHS source text (via ast unparse/segment or source slicing) from grounding.py and from the pre-move snapshot; assert byte-identical RHS. Explicitly assert the verification performs no git subprocess call and reads no commit hash.

criteria.description: wiki.py re-exports the relocated helpers and the shared WIKI_DIR constant via a one-way shim (e.g. 'from grounding import *' or explicit re-exports) so existing callers of wiki.py keep working unchanged. The shim import is the ONLY permitted structural addition to wiki.py.
criteria.verifyBy: Import wiki; assert every previously-public moved name (including WIKI_DIR and USAGE_INSTRUCTIONS) resolves to the grounding object (wiki.foo is grounding.foo). Assert the retained EXCLUDE function bodies in wiki.py (tool_schema, make_tool_handler) are byte-identical to the pre-move in-worktree snapshot, with the sole allowed diff being the added shim import line(s).

criteria.description: No wiki<->grounding circular import is introduced, and the shim is strictly ONE-WAY. Because system_prompt_block calls _load_index and reads USAGE_INSTRUCTIONS (all of which move together into grounding.py), no moved helper calls back into wiki. grounding.py MUST contain NO 'import wiki' of any form.
criteria.verifyBy: Static grep: assert grounding.py has no 'import wiki' / 'from wiki import' statement (one-way direction enforced). In a fresh interpreter import grounding then wiki; in another fresh interpreter reverse the order. Assert both succeed with no ImportError/partial-init error, and that calling one moved helper works after each import order.

criteria.description: build_system_instruction in bot.py receives a CODE-ACCURATE ruling separating BODY PURITY from MODULE-LEVEL IMPORT COUPLING. The ruling must cite: its body reads bot.py module globals (BASE_INSTRUCTION, WIKI_TAGLINE, WIKI_ENABLED, STUDY_BASE_INSTRUCTION, BREVITY_REMINDER, STUDY_REMINDER), calls wiki.system_prompt_block(), and calls load_profile/load_memory/load_most_recent_transcript_block; and DECISIVELY, bot.py performs eager top-level 'import anthropic' (~line 9) and top-level pipecat imports (~lines 13-35). The function BODY may be Pipecat/LLM/network-free and deterministic, but that alone does NOT make it includable. EXCLUDE is the expected correct ruling, justified by eager top-level anthropic+pipecat import coupling AND the wiki.system_prompt_block()/six-constant entanglement (which would create a c6 cycle if pulled into grounding.py). Any note describing the BODY as 'Pipecat-tangled' (it is not) is a FAILURE. If the generator instead rules INCLUDE, it MUST move all six constants into grounding.py, resolve the wiki.system_prompt_block cycle, re-export from bot.py, and prove grounding.py still imports with pipecat/anthropic uninstalled.
criteria.verifyBy: Statically inspect build_system_instruction's body for Pipecat/LLM/network references (expected: none) and its references to bot.py module globals and wiki.system_prompt_block (expected: present). If EXCLUDED: assert it still lives in bot.py and the one-line note names a concrete verifiable disqualifier — the eager top-level anthropic/pipecat import coupling and/or the constant+wiki.system_prompt_block entanglement — and reject any note misattributing in-body Pipecat/LLM calls. If INCLUDED: verify verbatim move (as c4), all six constants moved, bot.build_system_instruction is grounding.build_system_instruction, cycle resolved, and grounding.py imports with pipecat/anthropic absent.

criteria.description:
Hermetic characterization tests exist for EVERY moved helper, pinning today's input->output behavior with EXACT literals and covering BOTH branches of every conditional helper: (a) _load_index: INDEX.md present -> returns index_path.read_text() (assert the exact seeded text); absent -> returns exactly "" (empty string, NOT None); (b) system_prompt_block: index present -> returns exactly f"
## Matt's knowledge wiki

{index}

{USAGE_INSTRUCTIONS}" with the full verbatim USAGE_INSTRUCTIONS text asserted; index absent -> returns exactly None. Pin the actual current return values against the real code; weak assertions like assertIsNotNone are rejected.
criteria.verifyBy: A test module imports each moved helper from grounding and asserts concrete expected outputs. Assert _load_index absent-branch == "" and present-branch == the seeded file text; assert system_prompt_block present-branch equals the exact f-string including the full USAGE_INSTRUCTIONS literal and absent-branch is None. Assert at least one exact-literal assertion per branch per helper; reject 'is not None'-only assertions.

criteria.description: Tests are hermetic against network AND filesystem/home directory. Any LLM/network dependency is stubbed/faked. WIKI_DIR (= Path.home()/'second-brain'/'resources'/'wiki') is redirected by monkeypatching the LIVE attribute grounding.WIKI_DIR (the module where _load_index is DEFINED and which it reads at call time) to tmp_path — NOT wiki.WIKI_DIR, and not a stale local rebind. No test depends on the developer's real home or real ~/second-brain content. No baseline is keyed on git HEAD or commit state.
criteria.verifyBy: Run the suite with network disabled and no API keys; assert pass. Grep tests to assert: they monkeypatch grounding.WIKI_DIR (patched on the grounding module, not wiki) to tmp_path and seed/omit INDEX.md there; the present-branch test actually reads the tmp file; no reliance on real Path.home()/second-brain; no git subprocess calls; no commit-hash reads; no HEAD-derived snapshot used as an expected value.

criteria.description: The STT/TTS/Pipecat pipeline and LLM-backed recap functions in wiki.py remain untouched.
criteria.verifyBy: Diff wiki.py's non-moved regions (retained EXCLUDE functions, pipeline/recap code) against the pre-move in-worktree snapshot; assert byte-identical except for the single added shim import line(s) from c5.

criteria.description: bot.py's untouched surface is preserved: its LLM-backed functions (e.g. generate_session_summary, generate_session_analysis, generate_artifact), its pipeline entrypoint (e.g. bot()), and helpers like UsageAccumulator are unchanged. The only permitted bot.py edits are those required by the c7 ruling (verbatim removal of build_system_instruction + moved constants and a re-export shim IF included; otherwise ZERO edits to bot.py).
criteria.verifyBy: Diff bot.py against its pre-move in-worktree snapshot; assert all LLM/pipeline functions and UsageAccumulator are byte-identical. Assert the only diffs correspond exactly to the c7 decision (either none, or the documented move + shim).

criteria.description: The full test suite passes WITHOUT weakening the pre-existing suite. The concrete pre-existing test files (as they exist in tests/ today: test_session_state_*, test_documents_*, test_sanity, plus any others already present) remain present and are NOT deleted, skipped, or xfailed to force green. They may be edited ONLY where a moved import path legitimately requires it, and each such edit must be justified.
criteria.verifyBy: Enumerate the concrete pre-existing test files under tests/ before the sprint and assert each still exists after. Run the complete test runner (pytest, via uv per pyproject.toml/uv.lock) and assert exit code 0. Assert no pre-existing test was removed or newly marked skip/xfail relative to its pre-sprint state, except import-path edits necessitated by the relocation, which must be justified.

criteria.description: End-to-end shim proof: existing callers importing helpers via wiki.py still work against the re-export shim, transparently.
criteria.verifyBy: A test imports wiki (not grounding) and exercises at least one re-exported helper end-to-end, asserting the same concrete output as the grounding-imported version, proving the shim is transparent to existing callers.
